title: phyteuma betonicifolium subsp. scaposum
intro: Phyteuma betonicifolium subsp. scaposum, also known as the tufted rampion or rampion bellflower, is a flowering plant species in the Campanulaceae family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and act as a diuretic. The health benefits of Phyteuma betonicifolium subsp. scaposum include its potential to alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders, such as irritable bowel syndrome, and to support the urinary system. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be guided by a qualified healthcare professional.
